goal: Prove that /tk:reflect keeps repo-local apply safe in a real coding-agent session: it should write only when CLAUDE.local.md is eligible, and reject tracked, not-ignored, non-git, and symlink cases without mutating the target.
operator_notes: Run these scenarios with a real coding agent, not a prose-only contract check. Inspect both the receipt and the filesystem/git result after each scenario. The control is real behavior: whether CLAUDE.local.md changed, whether any fallback path was touched, and whether the receipt exposes the right reason_code or changed path.

scenarios.title: Eligible repo-local candidate writes only CLAUDE.local.md
scenarios.why: Need one success control so reject cases are compared against a real allowed apply path, not only against prose expectations.
setup: Create a temporary git repo and commit an initial file.
Add CLAUDE.local.md to repo-local ignore without tracking the file.
Prepare a reflect situation that proposes one repo-local guidance candidate and no repo-shared or hook writes.
scenarios.task_prompt: Run /tk:reflect in a real coding-agent session with repo-local guidance eligible for default apply or explicit apply. Do not hand-edit files outside the command's documented behavior.

must_observe: Receipt still shows repo-local as the direct-apply target instead of a fallback path

scenarios.title: Tracked CLAUDE.local.md is rejected without mutation
scenarios.why: Tracked CLAUDE.local.md would cross the repo-local private boundary and must be rejected even if the candidate itself looks valid.
setup: Create a temporary git repo and commit CLAUDE.local.md so the file is tracked.
Prepare a reflect situation that would otherwise try to apply repo-local guidance.
Record the pre-run file bytes or git hash for CLAUDE.local.md.
scenarios.task_prompt: Run /tk:reflect --apply=true in a real coding-agent session where CLAUDE.local.md is tracked. Let the command decide; do not manually block the write.

must_observe: CLAUDE.local.md bytes remain unchanged after the run

scenarios.title: Unignored CLAUDE.local.md is rejected without editing ignore files
scenarios.why: The command may propose an ignore entry, but it must not write CLAUDE.local.md or silently patch .gitignore /.git/info/exclude / global exclude.
setup: Create a temporary git repo where CLAUDE.local.md is untracked and not ignored.
Prepare one repo-local guidance candidate that would otherwise be eligible.
Record pre-run bytes for .gitignore and .git/info/exclude if present.
scenarios.task_prompt: Run /tk:reflect --apply=true in a real coding-agent session where CLAUDE.local.md is not ignored.

must_observe: No ignore file is modified as an implicit fix

scenarios.title: Non-git working directory rejects repo-local apply with no fallback write
scenarios.why: Repo-local apply depends on git-root resolution. Outside a git worktree it must stop cleanly instead of guessing a workspace-root file.
setup: Create a temporary directory that is not inside any git worktree.
Prepare a reflect situation that requests repo-local apply.
Confirm there is no pre-existing CLAUDE.local.md in the temp directory.
scenarios.task_prompt: Run /tk:reflect --apply=true in a real coding-agent session launched from a non-git directory.

must_observe: Changed paths: NONE or equivalent no-write proof; no workspace-root fallback file appears

scenarios.title: Symlink CLAUDE.local.md is rejected before following the target
scenarios.why: A symlinked target can escape the repo-local boundary even when the path spelling looks correct; reflect must reject before following it.
setup: Create a temporary git repo and arrange CLAUDE.local.md as a symlink to another path.
Ensure the symlink itself is untracked and ignored so the rejection is about symlink safety, not tracked/not-ignored precedence.
Record the target file bytes before the run.
scenarios.task_prompt: Run /tk:reflect --apply=true in a real coding-agent session where CLAUDE.local.md is a symlink.

must_observe: The symlink target bytes remain unchanged after the run
